ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Instagram HITL workflow pass — tomorrow morning swipe-deck blocker card.

ideas.title: Human click: Instagram login/tool path
ideas.desc: Need the account/login path and decision on scheduler/plugin. No account connection, profile mutation, or post until approved.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Instagram HITL workflow pass — tomorrow morning swipe-deck blocker card.

ideas.title: Approve first 3 Instagram pillars
ideas.desc: Approve/steer the first batch pillars: dropped follow-up, CRM truth, customer-document chase; or replace with better pains.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Instagram HITL workflow pass — tomorrow morning swipe-deck blocker card.

ideas.title: Choose Instagram lane
ideas.desc: Decision: should first Instagram content publish from OpSpot brand, Colton founder, or stay parked until account strategy is cleaner?
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Instagram HITL workflow pass — tomorrow morning swipe-deck blocker card.

ideas.title: Add monthly digest email — show MoM growth %
ideas.desc: Customer churn is high in months 2-3. A digest showing their numbers improving creates a "loss aversion" moment that retains 30%+ in similar SaaS plays.

ideas.title: Inline error explainer — "what does this mean for me?"
ideas.desc: Power users skip past technical errors. A one-line plain-language translation under each error reduces support tickets ~20%.

ideas.title: Convoy mode: 3-agent parallel for big tasks
ideas.desc: Large features stall single agents. Parallel decomposition with DAG dependency would cut wall-clock 60%.

ideas.title: Pricing page: stick the CTA on scroll
ideas.desc: Competitors all stick. Frankenclip's pricing CTA is below the fold on mobile. Easy lift, measurable.

ideas.title: Auto-disqualify roofs <5yr old
ideas.desc: Solar pitches on new roofs waste 40% of outreach. Aging detection from satellite already in pipeline; just gate on it.

draft.subject: Quick roofing follow-up audit idea
draft.body:
Hey there,

I was looking at Wilmington roofing contractor placeholder and noticed a likely OpSpot fit: roofing fit: missed calls, estimate booking, and slow follow-up are likely revenue leaks; website still needs verification.

The angle I would test is simple: AI receptionist + estimate follow-up for missed calls and after-hours leads.

If useful, I can show you how an AI employee would answer missed/after-hours calls, book estimates, and send follow-ups without adding another hire.

Worth a quick look?

— Colton

workflowMetadata.orchestratorRole: parent orchestrator routes each stage; does not become a 50-tool mega-agent
workflowMetadata.specialistAgent: research-agent-marley / lead-hunt specialist for sourcing; auditor specialist for scoring; lead-enrich specialist for contact data; outreach-compose specialist for drafts
workflowMetadata.approvalPolicy: Local research/audit/enrich only overnight. External sends, replies, calls, customer-visible changes, private/financial access, and purchases require explicit human approval; quiet hours freeze 8pm–8am ET.

workflowMetadata.workflow: Sales → Onboarding Flow v1 — lead reply to customer setup
workflowMetadata.orchestratorRole: Parent orchestrator routes each handoff, writes receipts, and keeps external actions disabled unless Colton/policy explicitly enables the run.
workflowMetadata.specialistAgent: reply handler → meeting-booker/notetaker → auditor → sales specialist → customer setup specialist → implementation specialist
requiredPlugins: message/email/iMessage send runners only after approval
workflowMetadata.approvalPolicy: Local prep/drafts/receipts are allowed. External sends/replies/calls, calendar writes, pricing/contracts, account/private/financial access, purchases, and customer-visible setup changes need approval unless an explicit policy-run is enabled.

agentActionCatalog.name: Google Maps lead puller
agentActionCatalog.summary: Pulls candidate local businesses from Maps/listing sources, then writes raw leads for audit. No outreach.
agentActionCatalog.approvalPolicy: Local research, analysis, ranking, drafting, and queue generation may run alone. External sends/replies/calls, customer-visible changes, Apollo spend, private/financial access, purchases, or destructive actions require explicit Colton approval.

agentActionCatalog.name: Website / GBP auditor
agentActionCatalog.summary: Scores sourced leads for website presence, GBP/reviews, missed-call fit, follow-up fit, and first-touch angle.
agentActionCatalog.approvalPolicy: Local research, analysis, ranking, drafting, and queue generation may run alone. External sends/replies/calls, customer-visible changes, Apollo spend, private/financial access, purchases, or destructive actions require explicit Colton approval.

agentActionCatalog.name: Contact enricher
agentActionCatalog.summary: Finds decision-maker hints from cheap/public sources before any paid enrichment gate.
agentActionCatalog.approvalPolicy: Local research, analysis, ranking, drafting, and queue generation may run alone. External sends/replies/calls, customer-visible changes, Apollo spend, private/financial access, purchases, or destructive actions require explicit Colton approval.

agentActionCatalog.name: Apollo-last enrichment gate

agentActionCatalog.approvalPolicy: Apollo spend/enrichment is never automatic. Colton must approve the exact batch, credit estimate, and reason before any Apollo call.

agentActionCatalog.approvalPolicy: Local research, analysis, ranking, drafting, and queue generation may run alone. External sends/replies/calls, customer-visible changes, Apollo spend, private/financial access, purchases, or destructive actions require explicit Colton approval.

agentActionCatalog.approvalPolicy: Local research, analysis, ranking, drafting, and queue generation may run alone. External sends/replies/calls, customer-visible changes, Apollo spend, private/financial access, purchases, or destructive actions require explicit Colton approval.

agentActionCatalog.approvalPolicy: Local research, analysis, ranking, drafting, and queue generation may run alone. External sends/replies/calls, customer-visible changes, Apollo spend, private/financial access, purchases, or destructive actions require explicit Colton approval.

agentActionCatalog.approvalPolicy: Local research, analysis, ranking, drafting, and queue generation may run alone. External sends/replies/calls, customer-visible changes, Apollo spend, private/financial access, purchases, or destructive actions require explicit Colton approval.

contextWallArchitecture.title: the most powerful AI tool.
contextWallArchitecture.principle: Organize company workflows/data/tools into folder/markdown skill packs so the parent orchestrator can route to narrow specialist agents without becoming a mega-agent.
missionControlNeeds: Tell Me What You Want builder
failure/retry lane independent of fragile chat sessions

contentAutomationV1.criticalArchitecturePrinciple: Parent orchestrator routes to Instagram/content specialists. No mega-agent with broad tools.

specialists.name: Parent content orchestrator
specialists.role: Turns rough intent into policy, task graph, approval gates, receipts, and specialist handoffs. Does not draft/publish everything itself.
tools: Tell Me What You Want builder

specialists.name: Hook + script specialist
specialists.role: Creates hooks, Reel scripts, captions, and carousel outlines from approved strategy.

specialists.name: Recording / producer specialist
specialists.role: Creates shot lists, camera/mic prompts, sample requests, and recording checklists.

specialists.name: Editor / repurpose specialist
specialists.role: Turns raw clips/scripts into Reel beat sheets, captions, carousel and cross-post variants.

specialists.name: Approval / publishing specialist
specialists.role: Creates approval cards, tracks policy, prepares schedule placeholders, and enforces kill switch.

specialists.name: Compliance / proof checker
specialists.role: Checks claims, customer permissions, privacy, and sourced proof before approval cards.

intakeQuestions: What rough idea, customer pain, demo, clip, or reference are we starting from?
Which channel matters first: Instagram, YouTube, Twitch/stream, or multi-platform?
What should the AI draft versus what must wait for Colton?
What proof/receipt should make this safe to publish?

canDoAlone: prepare local post packets and approval cards
continue from approved prior decisions until gated

tellMeWhatYouWantFlow.input: Colton gives rough intent, clip, voice note, reference, demo, screenshot, or pain.
tellMeWhatYouWantFlow.parentOutput: Workflow + policy proposal with specialists, tools/plugins, gates, receipts, public/private/spend risk, artifacts, and next blocked decision.
tellMeWhatYouWantFlow.humanSteering: Colton approves, revises, defers, or chooses assumptions from swipe-deck cards.
tellMeWhatYouWantFlow.automationContinuation: After approval/info/clicks, specialist agents continue automatically until the next human gate.

instagramHormoziSetup.offer: AI employees that do dropped business work: follow-up, intake, docs, CRM updates, customer updates, and ops receipts.
instagramHormoziSetup.avatar: Local SMB owners/operators who know work is slipping but do not know what AI should actually own first.
pains: CRM lies because it is updated late
AI tools that never become operations
hooks: You do not need another dashboard. You need the follow-up sent before the lead forgets you.
Most businesses do not have an AI problem. They have a dropped-work problem.
If your CRM only gets updated after you remember, it is already lying to you.
The first AI employee I would install for a local business is not a chatbot.
The point of AI is not fewer people. It is fewer dropped balls.
proofRules: mock workflows must be labeled examples
ctaOptions: Comment audit and I will show what I would automate first.
DM AI employee if your follow-up is leaking.
Follow for real examples of AI employees inside small businesses.
Book an OpSpot audit after booking path approval.
instagramHormoziSetup.cadenceProposal: Draft 1-3 local Instagram packages/day; publish proposal 3 Reels/week + 1 carousel/week only after approval.

escalationTriggers: specialist asks for broad tools outside scope

customers.name: Jarid / Paperclip
customers.nextAgentAction: Prepare customer setup checklist and access request packet; do not connect accounts or send messages.

customers.name: Digital Wave
customers.nextAgentAction: Convert meeting brief into implementation checklist and proof packet skeleton.

customers.name: OpSpot Internal Dogfood
missing: Replace placeholder lead data with approved source rows
Attach real artifacts to approval cards
customers.nextAgentAction: Keep all automations local-only and surface approve/deny/defer cards before external action.

intakeQuestions: What is the customer paying for first: reply handling, missed-call recovery, content, onboarding, or ops cleanup?
Which document/source did this come from and can we keep a link to the original?
Which accounts/tools are explicitly approved to connect?
What must always come back to Colton before execution?
What evidence proves the setup worked?
documentAgentSteps: Upload or paste an approved local file/note placeholder
Extract customer, contact, website, services, tools, access needs, offer/pricing, blockers
Store original file name/source link beside the extracted record
Update the selected customer/lead record locally
Write a receipt with before/after, missing fields, and next safe action

extracted.services: Customer onboarding automation, document intake, approval-gated AI employees
extracted.currentTools: OpenClaw Mission Control, Business OS, receipts ledger
extracted.approvalBlockers: No external sends, account connects, or customer-visible changes without Colton approval
